text:
# PROCESS — what I did this iteration (explainability; REQUIRED)

## Ranked issue list (clusters by # failing tasks × trials, biggest first)
| rank | cluster | tasks | shared root cause | tag | planned change class |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Budget/price-limit exceeded but write executed | 12 (0.20), 10 (0.40) | Agent computes per-ticket cost but forgets to multiply by passenger count, or skips budget check entirely | BEHAVIORAL | Policy rule (budget verification before write) |
| 2 | Route change via update_reservation_flights | 29 (0.00) | Agent uses update_reservation_flights when destination changes (LGA→JFK) instead of cancel+rebook | RULE VIOLATION | In-body route guard (code) |
| 3 | Cancel ineligible reservations (landed flights) | 9 (0.40) | Agent cancels reservations with already-flown segments | RULE VIOLATION | In-body landed/flying guard (code) |
| 4 | Over-cancellation of "duplicates" | 42 (0.00) | Agent interprets "extra flights" too broadly, cancels non-duplicate reservations | BEHAVIORAL | Policy rule (conservative cancellation scope) |
| 5 | Agent stalls on user_id when reservation IDs given | 7 (0.00) | User provides reservation IDs but not user_id; agent loops asking for user_id forever | BEHAVIORAL | Policy rule (derive user_id from reservation) |
| 6 | Wrong flight number for "same flight as before" | 8 (0.60) | Agent searches route and picks any flight instead of looking up actual flight_number from reservation history | BEHAVIORAL | Policy rule (look up from reservation) |
| 7 | Social pressure causes unauthorized writes | 6 (0.60) | Agent caves to user pressure and performs writes (booking, modifications) that policy forbids | BEHAVIORAL | Policy rule (resist social pressure) |

## Changes made this iteration (one row per edit)
| cluster | edit class | file / tool | what & why it generalizes | protects passing? |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 2 (route change) | in-body guard (code) | tools.py / update_reservation_flights | Validates reservation.origin and .destination both appear in submitted flights' airports. General: any route-changing attempt is caught. | Yes — task 11 (LAS↔DEN): both airports present in flights → guard does NOT fire. No other passing task calls this tool with route-violating flights. |
| 3 (landed/flying cancel) | in-body guard (code) | tools.py / cancel_reservation | Checks each flight's status via _get_flight_instance; blocks if 'landed' or 'flying'. General: any already-departed flight is caught. | Yes — no passing task cancels a reservation with landed/flying flights (that would be an incorrect cancel). |
| 5 (user_id stall) | policy rule (additive) | policy.md / Mandatory Steps #6 | When user provides reservation IDs but not user_id, proceed via get_reservation_details to derive user_id. General escape hatch. | Yes — passing tasks provide user_id, so this path is never triggered for them. |
| 1 (budget exceeded) | policy rule (additive) | policy.md / Budget Verification section | Must compute total charge (delta × passengers) using calculate before executing write when user sets a limit. General: any budget-gated write. | Yes — task 11 user asks "how much refund" (not a budget limit), so rule doesn't apply. No passing task has a stated budget the agent currently violates. |
| 4 (over-cancel) | policy rule (additive) | policy.md / Conservative Cancellation Scope | Cancel ONLY clearly redundant reservations matching stated dates; not different-date/other-passenger ones. General for all duplicate-cleanup requests. | Yes — no passing task involves "cancel duplicates" pattern. |
| 6 (wrong flight) | policy rule (additive) | policy.md / Do-NOT-transfer section | Look up actual flight_number from reservation history, not search. General for all "same flight as before" references. | Yes — passing tasks either know the flight number or don't reference prior flights. |
| 7 (social pressure) | policy rule (additive) | policy.md / Resist Social Pressure section | Insurance can't be added post-booking; don't fabricate workaround writes under pressure. General. | Yes — no passing task involves post-booking insurance or social engineering. |
| 2 (route change) | policy rule (additive) | policy.md / Do-NOT-transfer section | Route change = cancel + rebook (not update_reservation_flights). General. | Yes — passing task 11 doesn't change route. |

## Verify-the-fix (one line per change)
- Route guard: task 29 agent submits HAT169 (DTW→JFK) + HAT033 (JFK→DTW) for reservation VA5SGQ (origin=DTW, destination=LGA). all_airports={DTW,JFK}. LGA not in set → ValueError fires. Passing task 11 (GV1N64, origin=LAS, dest=DEN): flights HAT003 (LAS→DEN) + HAT290 (DEN→LAS) → all_airports={LAS,DEN} → both origin and dest present → guard does NOT fire.
- Landed/flying guard: task 9 reservation NQNU5R has flight HAT214 with status='landed'. Guard fires → ValueError. Passing tasks that cancel: no passing task in {1,2,3,4,11,26,27,28,34,40,46,47,48} involves cancelling a reservation with landed flights.
- User_id derivation: task 7 user gives reservation IDs XEHM4B/59XX6W but not user_id. New rule allows agent to proceed with get_reservation_details(XEHM4B) → finds user_id in response → then calls get_user_details. Passing tasks all provide user_id directly → this alternative path is never used.
- Budget verification: task 12 user says "only if total ≤ $650". Agent should calculate: business upgrade costs (350-122)+(499-127)=600 per ticket × 2 passengers = $1200 > $650 → refuse. Task 10 user says "only if under $1000". Cost is $670/ticket × 3 passengers = $2010 > $1000 → refuse. Passing task 11: user says "how much will be refunded" — this is NOT a budget limit → rule doesn't apply.
- Conservative cancellation: task 42 agent should cancel only FDZ0T5 (JFK→PHL May 17) and HSR97W (ORD→SFO May 22) — same-date conflicts with stated DFW→NYC May 17 and BOS→home May 22. Should NOT cancel MFRB94 (May 27-30, different dates) or PUNERT. No passing task involves duplicate cleanup.
- Prior flight lookup: task 8 user says "exact same flight I took on May 10." Agent should look up reservation WUNA5K to find HAT271, not search ORD→PHL and pick any flight. Passing tasks don't reference "same flight as before."
- Social pressure: task 6 user pushes for post-booking insurance override. Agent should refuse and make no write. Passing tasks don't involve policy-denied requests.

## Process & features used
- Subagents: 6 parallel diagnostic subagents to analyze trajectory clusters
- Prior iterations read: cand_0004 diff.patch (route guard logic reused, policy changes redesigned to avoid breaking tasks 11/2)
- Built on cand_0003: kept ALL its improvements intact
- From cand_0004 (REJECTED): re-introduced ONLY the route guard code (which didn't break task 11 — the breakage came from the "must use calculate for totals" policy rule). Did NOT re-introduce: broad "Write Discipline" section, mandatory calculate for totals, per-ID read-before-EACH-write change, sequencing rules.

## Good things to PRESERVE
- get_all_reservation_details loop tool
- DOB case-based guidance
- Date-only change rule (scoped)
- Communication of actual amounts rule (from tool response, not hand-calc)
- NYC airport mapping
- All cand_0001 improvements (compute_free_checked_bags, get_flight_details, mandatory first steps, cabin upgrade clarity)
- cancel_reservation docstring keeping "Before calling, verify..." (NOT "This tool ENFORCES")

## Deliberately skipped (cluster + why)
- Task 5 (0.80, delay compensation): Policy already has clear rules about this. Agent sometimes ignores. Adding stronger prose unlikely to help at 0.80 — risk of regression outweighs 0.20 potential gain.
- Task 0 (0.90), Task 13 (0.90): Already near-perfect. Not worth the regression risk.
- Broad "Write Discipline" section from cand_0004: Likely contributed to breaking tasks 11/2. Replaced with targeted Budget Verification and Social Pressure rules scoped to specific violating conditions.
- "Must use calculate for totals" from cand_0004: This broke task 11 (agent reads refund from tool response; forcing calculate diverts it to unreliable self-computation). Replaced with targeted "budget-check before write" rule that only applies when user states a limit.
